title: Baity / Beatty Deep Dive: Pre-1750 Origins to Pearl Mae Baity (Hunter Spence's maternal line)
methodology_note: Sources: NC Land Grants database (nclandgrants.com POST search, primary source); Courtney Baptist Church Cemetery Yadkin County and Deep Creek Baptist Church Cemetery Yadkin County (cemeterycensus.org, primary transcriptions); NCGenWeb 1830 Surry County census transcription; BeattyProject.org death index; Portal to Texas History (newspaper hits); SFA East Texas Research Center archival description; Wikipedia surname/county articles; HouseOfNames (citing Filby Passenger and Immigration Lists Index); SurnameDB (citing Reaney & Wilson). Prior repo files 46-baity-nc-colonial.md and 50-baity-integration-candidates.md integrated.
connection_to_hunter: Pearl Mae / Paralee Baity (b. 1878/1879) is Hunter's maternal great-great-grandmother. Her daughter Ruth Baity (b. ~1900) married Leroy Teichmuller Mattingly. Their child is Shari's father. Shari (Sharyn Mattingly) is Hunter's grandmother.

generations.label: Deepest possible NC Beatty — Land Grant 1753

person.full_name: Charles Beatty (and Thomas Beatty, joint grantee)
person.birth_year_note: adult by 1753; if arrived PA 1729, born c. 1700–1715
person.birth_place: POSSIBLE: County Down or County Tyrone, Ulster, Ireland → Pennsylvania c. 1729
key_facts: NC Land Grant: Charles Beatty and Thomas Beatty — 1 grant totaling 800 acres in 1753
Rowan County NC was established March 27, 1753 from Anson County — this grant is in the FIRST year of Rowan County's existence
HouseOfNames immigration record (citing Filby Passenger and Immigration Lists Index) lists 'Charles Beatty, who arrived in Pennsylvania in 1729'
Francis Beatty received 481 acres in Rowan County NC in 1759 — possibly a son or brother
These are PROBABLE family founders of the NC Baity/Beatty line

generations.notes: The 800-acre joint grant to two Beattys in the very first year of Rowan County is consistent with a family that had been staging in PA since 1729 and then moved south along the Great Wagon Road. Direct genealogical connection to the later Baity cluster in Surry/Yadkin is inferred from geography and surname, not yet proven by deed or probate.

generations.label: Colonial NC — Rowan/Surry corridor

person.birth_place: POSSIBLE: Pennsylvania or Virginia (pre-NC), settled Rowan/Surry County NC
key_facts: May 6, 1774: 'George Beate vs. Hugh Montgomery' — Rowan County Inferior Court minutes
April 2, 1785: Bond from Sylvester and John Thomson to sell George Batee 124 acres on Dutchman's Creek
1787: George Batey paying Rowan County taxes
January 19, 1798: Thomas Horn to George Baty, 300 acres on Chiquapin Creek near the Rowan line
1799–1800: David and William Batey taxed in the same district (likely his sons)
1800 census: George Beaty in Surry County NC
Surry County was formed from Rowan in 1771 — George's pre-1771 records are in Rowan
Possible wife: Rachel Allgood (cited in compiled genealogy; not confirmed by primary source)
sources: https://www.ncgenweb.us/alleghany/surry1830.html (1830 census transcription shows George Baity as head of household)

generations.notes: All documentary citations for George are from a compiled Ancestry sharing story, which summarizes Rowan/Surry court, deed, and tax records. The originals should be verified at NC State Archives (Raleigh) under Surry County and Rowan County collections. The NC Land Grants database confirms William Beatty received 5 grants (1,164 acres) in NC in 1789, consistent with the same cluster.

generations.label: Surry County NC — 1830 Census Generation

person.birth_year_note: adult head of household in 1830 Surry census; born c. 1770–1790
key_facts: Listed as separate head of household in 1830 Surry County census
Same census cluster as George Baity, Isam Baity, and Pleasant Baity
Generation fits as Isham's father (Isham b. 1804; David would be c. 25–35 in 1830)
Ancestry-compiled story lists David and William Batey as taxpayers in the same district 1799–1800

generations.notes: David → Isham parent-child link is NOT proven by a primary source in this pass. Required: 1810 or 1820 Surry County census showing David Baity with a son named Isham, OR a probate/deed record naming David as Isham's father.

generations.label: Earliest Cemetery-Confirmed NC Baity

person.burial: Courtney Baptist Church Cemetery, Yadkin County, NC
key_facts: PRIMARY SOURCE: Cemetery entry at Courtney Baptist Church Cemetery, Yadkin County NC — 'Baity, Isham "Isom" (b. 19 Oct 1804 - d. 23 Dec 1892)'
Wife Nancy Plowman confirmed by daughter Jennette C. Baity's cemetery entry: 'Daughter of Isham Baity and Nancy Plowman' at Deep Creek Baptist Church Cemetery
Known daughter: Jennette C. Baity (b. 13 Jan 1852, d. 17 May 1922), married Alexander Hutchens II
Known probable son: William D. Baity (b. c. 1829/1830, buried Courtney Baptist Cemetery)
Other probable children: John I. Baity (b. 1811), John Baity (b. 1813), M. L. Baity (b. 1805) — all in same cemetery cluster
sources: https://www.cemeterycensus.org/nc/yadk/cem026.htm (primary transcription, Deep Creek Baptist — Jennette entry)

generations.notes: This is the deepest genealogically confirmed Baity ancestor in the direct line (primary source: cemetery transcription). The parent-of-Isham link requires further primary research.

generations.label: Yadkin County — Mid-19th Century

person.birth_year_note: c. 1829/1830 — cemetery census shows b. 1830; PeopleLegacy gives 1829
person.death_year_note: PeopleLegacy burial index gives 1829–1894; cemetery census has only birth year
person.burial: Courtney Baptist Church Cemetery, Yadkin County, NC
key_facts: Cemetery entry: 'Baity, William D (b. 1830 - d. Unknown)' at Courtney Baptist Church Cemetery
PeopleLegacy burial index independently gives dates 1829–1894 at same cemetery
Probable father: Isham 'Isom' Baity (generation fits; same cemetery cluster)
Probable son: William Alexander Baity (Pearl's father; generation fits)

generations.notes: William D. to Isham parent-child link needs a primary census record or deed to confirm. William D. to William Alexander link is from prior repo work (files 46 and 50) and also needs a direct primary source.

generations.label: NC-to-Texas Migration Generation

person.birth_year_note: c. 1850s based on generation; born NC
key_facts: Known from prior repo work as Pearl/Paralee's husband; W. A. Baity in all Texas records
San Antonio builder and developer 1909–1926 (multiple newspaper citations)
Occupation confirmed: owner of 'Fair Play Home Company' (1909), developer of Highland Terrace (1919), 'Blue Ribbon Homes' (1924–1926)
Residence: 211 Castillo Ave., San Antonio (1925)
Baity surname from Yadkin County NC per prior research

generations.notes: The full expansion of W. A. to 'William Alexander' is from prior repo work (46-baity-nc-colonial.md) and not independently re-confirmed in this pass. A Texas death certificate image or Texas marriage record would confirm.

generations.label: Hunter's Maternal Great-Great-Grandmother

person.birth_date_note: 26 Jul 1878 per death-index aggregator; 1879 per Shari's oral account
person.birth_place: North Carolina (per oral history — moved to TX as a child)
key_facts: Death index (BeattyProject.org): 'Paralee Johnson Baity, died 12-26-1969, BEXAR, female, widowed'
Bexar County deed Nov. 2, 1910: 'W. A. Baity and Paralee Baity to W. F. Daniel...'
Known daughter: Ruth Baity (b. ~1900, d. 1982) who married Leroy Teichmuller Mattingly
Oral history (Shari): Pearl came from NC to TX as a child, bought Reeves County land April 1901 retaining mineral rights (Wolfcamp Shale)
Nickname 'Pearl' used by family; legal name was Paralee

generations.notes: Direct connection to Hunter: Pearl → Ruth Baity → Leroy T. Mattingly marriage → Shari's father → Shari → Hunter's parent → Hunter. Pearl is Hunter's maternal great-great-grandmother.

generations.label: Ruth Baity — Bridge to Mattingly Line

person.spouse: Leroy Teichmuller Mattingly (b. 1898, La Grange TX)
key_facts: Daughter of Paralee/Pearl Baity
Married Leroy Teichmuller Mattingly — bridging Baity and Mattingly family lines
Mother of Shari Mattingly's father

generations.notes: Confirmed from oral history and Mattingly lineage file. No independent primary source found in this pass.

surname_origin.etymology: Beatty derives from 'Bate' or 'Batey', diminutive/pet forms of Bartholomew. Not from 'Beatrice' as often assumed. Classified as a 'Border surname' found on both sides of Scotland/England boundary.
surname_origin.american_spelling_evolution: The 'Baity' spelling is a Southern American phonetic rendering of 'Beaty/Beatie'. NC records show progression: Batee (1774) → Baty (1785) → Batey (1787) → Baity (1800s) → Baity (standard by 1830).
surname_origin.ulster_concentration: County Down (especially Ards Peninsula / Strangford Lough area), County Tyrone, County Antrim, County Fermanagh — settled during Plantation of Ulster 1610–1640

path.context: Plantation of Ulster — Scots settlers receive land in County Down, Tyrone, Fermanagh
Mass Scots-Irish emigration via Londonderry/Belfast to Philadelphia; 'Charles Beatty arrived Pennsylvania 1729' per Filby
Great Wagon Road migration south from PA Dutch/Scots-Irish settlements
Yadkin Valley settlers arriving; Rowan County formed 1753; Charles and Thomas Beatty 800-acre grant in year of county formation
Surry County split from Rowan 1771; George Baity cluster in Surry by 1800
William Alexander Baity moves to Texas; Pearl Baity moves as a child per oral history
W. A. Baity and Pearl/Paralee established in San Antonio by 1909

grants.notes: Earliest documented Beatty in NC; Rowan County first year of existence
Possibly son or brother of the 1753 grantees
5 grants in same corridor — likely same family cluster
Wm A. = William Alexander? Same initials as Pearl's husband
First 'Baity' spelling in land grants database

courtney_baptist_cemetery_full_baity_lis.note: Full confirmed burials of Baity surname at Courtney Baptist Church Cemetery, Yadkin County NC

key_entries.name: Hutchens, Jennette C. Baity
key_entries.note: Wife of Alexander Hutchens II. CONFIRMED: Daughter of Isham Baity and Nancy Plowman.

key_entries.name: Baity, Enoch Alexander
key_entries.note: Son of Pleasant Baity and Harriett Steelman. Married Sarah J. Sprinkle 1854.

key_entries.name: Hoots, Genette Emiline Allgood
key_entries.note: Daughter of John W. Allgood and Mary Jane Baity — confirms Baity-Allgood intermarriage in this cluster

key_entries.name: Caudle, Eliza Belle Baity Hoots
key_entries.note: Daughter of G. W. Baity and Abigail Hauser

civil_war_service.note: All from 28th NC Infantry, Company I — Yadkin County

open_research_gaps: George → David → Isham chain: needs 1810/1820 Surry County census image showing David Baity with son Isham, OR probate/deed record
1753 Charles/Thomas Beatty → George Baity connection: needs Rowan County deed/court records 1753–1774 at NC State Archives
William D. Baity → William Alexander Baity parent-child link: needs 1870 or 1880 NC census showing William Alexander in a Baity household
William Alexander Baity Texas death certificate: would confirm NC birth county and parents
Pearl/Paralee Reeves County April 1901 land purchase: needs Texas GLO or Reeves County deed record
Ulster/Scotland origin pre-1700: requires ScotlandsPeople paid search or PRONI (Belfast) paid records; free indexes do not go back far enough for this family's probable 1650–1700 origin window
